data.task:
Atom 0 of ticket 0070 (AC-3 second half) — DAEMON read side only. Build the daemon surface that lists PENDING runnerless handoffs so they are discoverable from creation until their first run record lands. UI is a later atom; do NOT touch packages/ui in this atom.

WHAT EXISTS NOW: `requestIndependentHandoff` in packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts:459-522 writes a markdown artifact to `local/runnerless-handoffs/<workspaceId>/<handoffId>.md` and emits an `emitOzEvent(... type:'runnerless-handoff', status:'handoff-created')` that currently has ZERO consumers. The artifact body contains lines `- Priority: <priorityId>`, `- Created: <ISO timestamp>`, `- Workspace: <name> (<id>)`. Run records are listed via the store's `listRuns({ workspaceId })` (packages/core/src/store/types.ts), and each Run has `workspaceId`, `priorityId`, `createdAt`, `status`.

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. A function (place it in packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts next to requestIndependentHandoff, exported) e.g. `listPendingRunnerlessHandoffs(ctx, workspaceId)` that:
   - Reads the directory `local/runnerless-handoffs/<workspaceId>/` under ctx.cocoderHome (gracefully returns [] if the dir does not exist).
   - For each `*.md` artifact, parses the `- Priority:` and `- Created:` lines to recover the exact priorityId and creation ISO timestamp. Do NOT derive priorityId from the handoff filename (it is lossy/sanitized) — parse it from the artifact body, which is the source of truth.
   - Cross-checks against `ctx.store.listRuns({ workspaceId })`: a handoff is PENDING (still discoverable) when NO run record exists for that priorityId yet. Once a run record for that priorityId exists, the handoff is no longer pending and is omitted.
   - Returns an array of `{ workspaceId, priorityId, handoffPath (repo-relative, same shape requestIndependentHandoff returns), createdAt (epoch ms) }`, newest-first.
2. A GET endpoint `GET /workspaces/:id/pending-handoffs` registered in `dispatchReads()` in packages/daemon/src/routes.ts (follow the exact structure of the sibling reads like `/workspaces/:id/priorities` and `/runs`), returning `{ handoffs: [...] }` with a 404 for an unknown workspace, matching how the neighboring read endpoints handle unknown workspace.

ACCEPTANCE / EXIT CRITERION:
- Add daemon tests in packages/daemon/tests/read-surfaces.test.ts (follow the existing `get(oz, path)` helper + `:memory:` store pattern) proving: (a) a handoff artifact with NO matching run record is returned by GET /workspaces/:id/pending-handoffs; (b) once a run record for that priorityId exists in the store, the same handoff is NOT returned; (c) unknown workspace → 404; (d) a workspace with no handoffs dir → empty list, 200.
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` (or the repo's daemon test command — discover it from package.json) passes, and `pnpm typecheck` (or equivalent) is clean for the daemon + core packages.

MUST NOT BREAK: do not change requestIndependentHandoff's existing behavior, the artifact format, or any existing route. Do not touch packages/ui. Match surrounding style; keep the listing function pure/read-only. Report the exact test command you ran and its real exit output as evidence.

data.reason: Duplicate parallel impls of one feature: launcher.ts AND routes.ts each define listPendingRunnerlessHandoffs + a PendingRunnerlessHandoff interface, with two endpoints (/runnerless-handoffs and /pending-handoffs); also edited ticket file + packages/ui, which the atom forbade.

data.task:
Atom 0 (RETRY) of ticket 0070 (AC-3 second half) — DAEMON read side only. A prior attempt was rejected for creating TWO parallel implementations and TWO endpoints for one feature, and for editing files outside scope. Read these constraints carefully.

GOAL: discover PENDING runnerless handoffs — artifacts under `local/runnerless-handoffs/<workspaceId>/<id>.md` that have no corresponding run record yet — via a single daemon read endpoint.

EXACTLY ONE OWNER, EXACTLY ONE ENDPOINT — non-negotiable:
1. Implement ONE exported function in packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts, named `listPendingRunnerlessHandoffs(ctx, workspaceId)`, with ONE small private helper for parsing the artifact body. Define the result interface ONCE (e.g. `PendingRunnerlessHandoff`) in launcher.ts and export it. Do NOT define a second copy of this interface anywhere else — routes.ts must IMPORT it from launcher.ts, not redeclare it.
2. Register ONE endpoint: `GET /workspaces/:id/pending-handoffs` in `dispatchReads()` in packages/daemon/src/routes.ts. The route handler must be a thin wrapper that resolves the workspace (404 on unknown, matching sibling reads like `/workspaces/:id/priorities`) and returns `{ workspace, handoffs }` by calling the launcher function. The handler must contain NO parsing or cross-check logic of its own — all of that lives in the launcher function. Do NOT add any second route (no `/runnerless-handoffs` route, no duplicate handler).

FUNCTION BEHAVIOR:
- Read dir `local/runnerless-handoffs/<workspaceId>/` under ctx.cocoderHome; if it does not exist (ENOENT) return [].
- For each `*.md` file, parse the artifact BODY (not the filename) for the `- Priority: <id>` and `- Created: <ISO>` lines — those are the source of truth (the filename is sanitized/lossy). Skip any file that lacks a parseable `- Priority:` line.
- Cross-check `ctx.store.listRuns({ workspaceId })`: a handoff is PENDING (included) when NO run record exists for that priorityId yet; once a run record for that priorityId exists, OMIT it.
- Return `{ workspaceId, priorityId, handoffPath (repo-relative, same shape requestIndependentHandoff returns), createdAt }`, newest-first.

TESTS (exit criterion): extend packages/daemon/tests/read-surfaces.test.ts following the existing `get(oz, path)` + `:memory:` store pattern, proving via the ONE endpoint `GET /workspaces/:id/pending-handoffs`: (a) a handoff artifact with NO matching run record is returned; (b) once a run record for that priorityId exists, the same handoff is NOT returned; (c) unknown workspace -> 404; (d) workspace with no handoffs dir -> 200 with empty list.

STRICT SCOPE — touch ONLY: packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts, packages/daemon/src/routes.ts, packages/daemon/tests/read-surfaces.test.ts. DO NOT touch packages/ui. DO NOT edit any cocoder/tickets/** file. DO NOT change requestIndependentHandoff, the artifact format, or any existing route or unrelated test. If an existing test legitimately needs updating because you added a route, explain why in your report rather than silently editing unrelated test files.

EVIDENCE: run the daemon test command (discover it from package.json, e.g. `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test`) and `pnpm typecheck`; report the exact commands and their real exit output.
